events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: Lockout/Tagout work, observed field condition is "energy source missed (stored hydraulic/gravity/capacitor)." This is a serious LOTO violation under OSHA 1910.147, which requires all energy sources including stored energy to be isolated, dissipated, and verified. Missing stored energy sources (hydraulic, gravity, capacitor) is a critical safety hazard.

Decision: fail
Risk: critical
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: non-compliant, installed-defective
Percent complete: maybe 80
Confidence: high, 0.95
Findings: stored energy sources not isolated/dissipated
Actions: immediately halt work, isolate and dissipate all stored energy sources, re-verify zero energy state
References: OSHA 1910.147

"actions": [
"Immediately halt work in the affected area and clear personnel from potential release paths",
"Identify and isolate all stored energy sources including hydraulic accumulators, gravity-loaded components, and capacitive charges",
"Apply lockout/tagout devices to all energy-isolating means and dissipate or restrain stored energy",
"Perform and document zero-energy verification before resuming work",
"Revise the LOTO procedure to include all stored energy sources and retrain affected personnel"
],
"rationale": "OSHA 1910.147 requires isolation, dissipation, and verification of all hazardous energy, including stored energy such as hydraulic, gravity, and capacitor sources. Missing any stored energy source means the LOTO is incomplete and the equipment is not in a safe zero-energy state, creating a critical risk of unexpected energization or release.",
"references": [
"OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 - The Control of Hazardous Energy (Lockout/Tagout)",
"OSHA 1910.147(d)(4)(ii) - release or restraint of stored energy",
"OSHA 1910.147(d)(5) - verification of isolation"
]
